info.description:
The 'Agreement' integration focuses on allowing 3rd party systems to sync contacts / properties from Palace and create new 'Owners', 'Properties', 'Tenancies' and 'Sub-Tenants'. It also allows for sending official 'Agreements' back to Palace as a file document (PDF Recommended).

The 'Agreement Integration' area does NOT require an upgrade from a 'Standard' API account to a 'Premium' API Account. There is a NO cost involved in using these contracts unless you are a 3rd Party solution provider wanting to integrate at a partner level.

Along with the CRM contracts, the system also includes an 'Available Property' contracts for those specifically wanting to display properties that available to tenanted only.

NOTE: For supporting contracts to obtain further codes for the CRM insert functionality please review the CRM Contracts available at https://app.swaggerhub.com/apis-docs/Palace/CRM_Integration/2.0.0


UPDATE: 18th December, 2025

The following endpoints have been updated to automatically to include a new field for Pet Bonds Required within a 'Tenancy' (TenancyPetBondRequired)

  • v2DetailedTenancy/JSON/{TenancyCode}
  • v2ViewAllDetailedTenancy/JSON
  • v2ViewAllDetailedTenancyByProperty/JSON/{PropertyCode}
  • v2InsertTenancy/JSON
  • v2UpdateTenancy/JSON/JSON


    UPDATE:30th September, 2025

    The following endpoints have been updated to automatically exclude 'archived' tenants. This is an extension from an earlier update in Palace, allowing customers to archive 'additional tenants. Now when you call any of the below endpoints, you will only receive 'current/active' tenants from the desired database

  • v2DetailedTenancy/JSON/RBTGXXXXXX
  • v2ViewAllDetailedTenancyByProperty/JSON/RBPRXXXXXX

    For those using v2ViewAllDetailedTenancy/JSON you will continue to 'GET' ALL tenants (current & archived), however, you can filter the archived entries out by making a slight adjustment to the endpoint via v2ViewAllDetailedTenancy/JSON?current=yes

    Update: 11th March 2025 - 'Tenancies' contracts now have the 'Status' exposed (TenancyStatus) to check for Active, Inactive and Debtors...

    v2ViewAllDetailedTenancy/JSON
    v2DetailedTenancy/JSON
    v2ViewAllDetailedTenancyByProperty/JSON
    v2InsertTenancy/JSON
    v2UpdateTenancy/JSON

    Update: 10th November 2024 - New 'Tenancy Termination' fields exposed…
    v2DetailedTenancy/JSON
    v2ViewAllDetailedTenancy/JSON
    v2ViewAllDetailedTenancyByProperty/JSON

    Update: 13th May 2024 - New Gas & Power Consumption exposed...
    V2InsertProperty/JSON
    V2UpdateProperty/JSON

post.description:
Inserts 'Diary' entry with external 'File' attached.

Returns Code of 'Inserted Entry' if successful...
e.g. Diary Insert (Successful) - Code: RBRE000018245

Inserts 'Diary' entry as an external 'URL Link'.

Returns Code of 'Inserted Entry' if successful...
e.g. Diary Insert (Successful) - Code: RBRE000018245

PropertyAdvert.example: Great place to live!  You'll love it!

PropertyKeepBackReason.example: Saving to replace tiles on the roof.

PropertyKeyAccess.example: Key is at the office.  Please ask at reception.

TenancyNote.example: This is a really good tenancy that pays on time every time!

SupplierComments.example: This plumber is one of the best in auckland

PropertyChangeCode.description: Code indicating how many times this record has been updated.  The code is incremented by 1 on each update.  This can also be used to track if the record has been updated since the last request.

PropertyCode.description: The 'Internal Code' of the 'Property'.  This code is only unique within the client database.  It is recommended that you use 'PropertyCodeGlobal' if you are making the data public or consuming property information from multiple client databases.

PropertyCodeGlobal.description: The 'Global Code' of the 'Property'.  This code is unique across ALL Palace databases.  It is recommended that you use 'PropertyCodeGlobal' if you are making the data public or consuming property information from multiple client databases.

PropertyDateAvailable.description: Date the property is available from.  This will be formatted as yyyy-MM-dd

PropertyGrid.description: Generally is used as in 'Internal' reference for a 'Map Grid' for grouping purposes.

PropertyManagementType.description: A description of the 'Management Type' of the property.  Although these descriptions can be defined by the user, Palace has a 'Pattern Criteria' definiting 4 specific types of 'Managements'. Descriptions containing the word 'Manage' specifies a residential management.  Descriptions containing the word 'Casual' specifies a casual property. Descriptions containing the word 'Commercial' specifies a commercial management.  Descriptions containing the word 'Holiday' specifies a holiday management

PropertyName.description: This is user defined 'Free Form' text giving a name to a property (NOTE: This is not the same as the address of the property)

PropertyOwnerCode.description: Internal code assigned to the 'Owner' of the property.

PropertyRentAmount.description: This is the amount of the rent.

PropertyRentalPeriod.description: This is related to the the 'PropertyRentAmount' field (e.g. 200 / Week).  The 'Rental Period' indicates how often the rent should be paid on this property.  Options are Day, Week, Fortnight, Month

PropertySortCode.description: This is a user defined code for sorting.  Generally, this will be the same as the 'Street Addres' name

PropertyStatus.description: This value will be 'Active' or 'Inactive' and indicates if the 'Property' is an active management at this time (NOTE: An 'Inactive' management may suggest the 'Property' is having work done at this time and cannot currently be rented)

PropertyUnit.description: The 'Unit' of the 'Property'.  If displaying the full 'Property Address' and a 'Unit' exists, the system should display 'Unit / PropertyAddress1' (e.g. 1/15 Stuart Street).

PropertyExternalCodes.description: External Codes provided by 3rd parties that require references to their own services

v2AvailablePropertyCustomList.description: Companies can elect to have extra field names and values that are setup specifically for their company. These can be diplayed in this 'Custom List' array with a related 'Name' and 'Value' entry. If none are setup this will return an empty array

v2AvailablePropertyFeatures.description: These are a specific set of 'Feature' entries avaiable for each property.

PropertyAdvertText.example: Come and see this great property in the heart of the city...

PropertyClass.description: This will be one of the following options (Apartment, House, Home and Income, Lifestyle, Unit, Townhouse, Holiday Home, Section Res, Flats).

PropertyFeatureDetails.description: Free form list of other features

PropertyFurnishings.description: Free form list of furnishings within the property

PropertyGeographicLocation.description: latitude and longitude separted by : (From Google Maps API)

PropertyHeader.example: New Property for Rent (Be Quick, don't miss out!)

PropertyNewConstruction.description: Is the 'Property' a new construction (Yes / No)

PropertyParking.description: This can be a number or free form text

PropertyPetsAllowed.description: Is the 'Property' allowed to have pets (Yes / No)

PropertyPublishAddress.description: Should the 'Property Address' be published online

PropertyPublishEntry.description: Should the 'Property' entry be published online

PropertySmokersAllowed.description: Is the 'Property' allowed smoking on premise (Yes / No)

PropertyStories.description: Number of Storeys of the property

PropertyWebLinkURL.description: Link to website or listing URL

PropertySuburbCode.description: External Code Reference (NOTE: Based to 'Suburb Type')

PropertySuburbDistrictOrPostcode.description: Suburb District (NOTE: In 'Australia' this displays a 'Post Code')

PropertySuburbType.description: The 'Suburb Type' is describes where the source of the suburb code originates

OwnerPrimaryAgentCode.description: Code can be obtained from '/v2ViewAllDetailedAgent/JSON' contract.

DiaryType.description: This field has the following limited options (owner, property, tenancy, supplier). In the case of an Inspection App supplying an Inspection Report, the input should be 'property'.

DiaryTypeCode.description: This is dependent on the DiaryType entry and should relate to the foreign key of the associated entity (i.e. if the DiaryType is a property the DiaryTypeCode should be the primary key of the associated property)

DiaryFileExtension.description: This is the file extension of the associated document.  In the case of a 'Mobile Inspection Report' we recommend a PDF

DiaryDescription.description: This is the descrption associated with the 'Diary Entry'.  For an inspection report we recommend using the description 'Inspection Report'

DiaryAgentCode.description:
'Agent Code' associated with diary (In the case of a 'Mobile Inspection' this should be the Property Manager that performs the inspection)

Code can be obtained from '/v2ViewAllDetailedAgent/JSON' contract.

DiaryGroupCode.description:
This is related to the 'Diary Group' field which is used to categorize diary entries. For Inspection Reports please use SYS001 which is a system code that relates to inspections within Palace.

Code can be obtained from '/v2ViewAllDetailedDiaryGroup/JSON' contract.

DiaryOnline.description: This should be the string value 'True' or 'False'.  This decides if the inspection is displayed on the 'Client' portal so 'Owners' can see inspections that Property Managers have completed.

DiaryImage.description: This is a Base64 encoded string of the document attached to the diary

DiaryType.description: This field has the following limited options (owner, property, tenancy, supplier). In the case of an Inspection App supplying an Inspection Report, the input should be 'property'.

DiaryTypeCode.description: This is dependent on the DiaryType entry and should relate to the foreign key of the associated entity (i.e. if the DiaryType is a property the DiaryTypeCode should be the primary key of the associated property)

DiaryHyperlink.description: This is a URL link to an external source.  For the workflow to work correctly on a general basis, this should refer to an 'Unlisted' link rather than a 'Private' on requiring authentication

DiaryDescription.description: This is the descrption associated with the 'Diary Entry'.  For an inspection report we recommend using the description 'Inspection Report'

DiaryAgentCode.description:
'Agent Code' associated with diary (In the case of a 'Mobile Inspection' this should be the Property Manager that performs the inspection)

Code can be obtained from '/v2ViewAllDetailedAgent/JSON' contract.

DiaryGroupCode.description:
This is related to the 'Diary Group' field which is used to categorize diary entries.

Code can be obtained from '/v2ViewAllDetailedDiaryGroup/JSON' contract.

DiaryOnline.description: This should be the string value 'True' or 'False'.  This decides if the inspection is displayed on the 'Client' portal so 'Owners' can see inspections that Property Managers have completed.
